# Emiliano G.O. — Portfolio

> Software engineer building Python backends, CLI tools, and data infrastructure.
> Based in Montevideo, Uruguay.

## https://emiliano-go.com/

### Hero

Backend Engineer · Data Scientist

Building systems that handle real data.

Python developer focused on data pipelines, ETL infrastructure, and backend architecture. Based in Montevideo, Uruguay. Open to interesting problems.

Stats:
- ~2K stars on maintained OSS
- 6 packages on PyPI
- Montevideo, Uruguay

### About

Emiliano G.O.
Montevideo, Uruguay

Backend developer and data scientist. I work mostly in Python: pipelines, APIs, and the infrastructure that keeps them running. I care about clean architecture, good documentation, and building things that hold up under load.

I learn by building things until they break, then figuring out why. Also run LibreCourseUY, an open source organization promoting the OSS ecosystem in Uruguay.

Stack: Python, FastAPI, pandas, scikit-learn, matplotlib, PostgreSQL, Prefect, Redis, Docker, Traefik, Vue 3, Taskiq.

### Projects (Home)

#### DBWarden

SQL-first migration system for SQLAlchemy. No migration runtime, no generated Python scripts. Built-in rollback, schema snapshots, offline CI generation, pre-deploy impact analysis, multi-database support, and a full CLI.

Tags: SQLAlchemy, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, 5 dialects
Links: GitHub, PyPI

#### vigil

Prefect-based GitHub activity monitor. Fetches events, commits, and PR activity via PyGithub, stores in ClickHouse.

### LibreCourseUY

An open source organization focused on promoting the FOSS ecosystem in Uruguay. We build and maintain free tools, services, and infrastructure for the Uruguayan developer community.

Links: GitHub donation

### Open Source

#### FastAPI-boilerplate (Co-maintainer)

Batteries-included async FastAPI starter shipping auth (sessions, OAuth, API keys), FastCRUD, Alembic, Taskiq workers, Redis caching, and per-tier rate limiting. Organized as a uv workspace with vertical-slice layout. ~2k stars, 286 forks.

## https://emiliano-go.com/projects

### DBWarden

Python Package · PyPI. The SQL-first database toolkit for SQLAlchemy.

DBWarden is a SQL-first migration system for SQLAlchemy that replaces Python-based migration workflows with explicit, reviewable SQL generated directly from your models. Unlike script-based migration tools, DBWarden does not introduce a migration runtime or require executing generated Python migration code. It produces plain SQL files that can be reviewed, versioned, and executed directly against any environment.

Stats: 12 stars, 829 tests, 31K+ LOC, 5 DB dialects, 15+ CLI commands.

Features: SQL-first migration system, multi-database support (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, MariaDB, MSSQL), offline CI generation, pre-deploy impact analysis, schema snapshots and diff, rollback support, declarative model-driven configuration.

Links: GitHub, PyPI, Documentation, DeepWiki

### schemap

Automatic Pydantic v2 schemas from SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM models. Define your model once, schemap generates Create, Update, Public, and Full schemas automatically, with field-level type mapping, nullability handling, and configurable exclusions.

Supports AutoBase inheritance and @auto_schema decorator, with built-in TimestampMixin and SoftDeleteMixin.

Tags: Pydantic v2, SQLAlchemy 2.0, PyPI

### seoslug

Deterministic SEO payload generation for content platforms. Pure functions, framework-agnostic. Generates canonical URLs, Open Graph (10+ fields), Twitter Cards (6+ fields), robots directives, and JSON-LD structured data. URL normalization with tracking parameter stripping.

Fast: 10,000 payloads in ~276 ms.

Tags: Python, PyPI, SEO

Links: GitHub, PyPI

### PyPI Utilities

#### seoslug (also featured above)

Deterministic SEO payload generation: canonical URLs, Open Graph, Twitter Cards, and JSON-LD. Same input always produces the same output. Testable, cacheable, predictable.

Links: GitHub, PyPI

#### detrack

Strip tracking parameters from URLs. Deterministically. Zero dependencies. 60+ default patterns, case-insensitive, pure functions, metadata returned.

Links: GitHub, PyPI

#### acrresolv

Acronym resolver for Python. Converts natural language phrases to acronyms. Zero dependencies, CLI and library usage.

Links: GitHub, PyPI

### More Projects

#### crxml

Fast streaming parser for Crystal Reports XML exports with Rust acceleration. Streams row-by-row without loading the full document into memory. Built-in pipeline stages: rename, cast types, filter, and drop fields. Parallel mode for multi-core systems. Outputs DataFrames via pandas, CSV, or dicts.

Tags: Python, Rust, XML, pandas
Links: GitHub, PyPI

#### vigil

Personal GitHub activity monitoring workflow built with Prefect. Fetches events, commits, and PR activity via PyGithub, stores structured records in ClickHouse through DBWarden's migration framework. Cron-scheduled execution with structured logging and error notifications.

Tags: Python, Prefect, PyGithub, ClickHouse
Links: GitHub

#### mapcreator

Tile-based building map editor built with React and Canvas. Multi-floor building layouts with grid editor, A* pathfinding across floors, 60fps canvas rendering with virtual scrolling, undo/redo, JSON export.

Tags: React, TypeScript, Canvas, A* Pathfinding
Links: GitHub

#### clickvault

HashiCorp Vault database secrets engine plugin for ClickHouse. Generates ephemeral ClickHouse users on demand (dynamic secrets) and rotates static role passwords on a schedule. Implements Vault database plugin SDK. All DDL isolated in a dedicated SQL layer. Clustered deployments get ON CLUSTER appended automatically.

Tags: Go, Vault, ClickHouse, Plugin
Links: GitHub

## Tech Stack

Astro, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript. Deployed as static site to Cloudflare Pages.
